signals.title: NZD/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + CESI Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5877 anchors the week's dominant G8 theme as the USD leader (+0.538%) faces the NZD laggard (-0.818%). The 1.355% spread is driven by a stark data divergence: USD CESI shows a +26 net beat (strong positive) while NZD remains in a negative bias. Despite the 150bp USD carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25%), NZD COT LF net short -13,767 suggests specs are already positioned for this weakness, though retail remains 78% long (contrarian bearish). Implication: structural NZD underperformance persists until CESI momentum shifts.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + Options Skew
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.99 tests the critical 160.00 threshold amid BoJ tightening signals and a massive +290bp OIS carry advantage (SOFR 3.63% vs TONA 0.73%). While JPY COT LF net short -86,249 shows crowded carry positioning, the options market is aggressively hedging the downside with a -1.15 25d RR put skew. This divergence between spot carry demand and options protection suggests high sensitivity to BoJ verbal intervention at 160.00. Implication: carry remains the primary driver but tail-risk hedging is accelerating.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Volatility + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7142 shows a significant multi-layer divergence. COT LF net long +58,041 (76.1% long) represents a crowded institutional long, while retail is 70% short, fading the move. This positioning tension is met with an extreme +8.4% Vol Risk Premium (IV 16.5% vs HV30 8.1%), indicating the options market is pricing in significantly more realized vol than currently observed. Despite the 60bp AUD carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%), the negative CESI (-8 misses) and hawkish Bullock tone suggest a 'sell the fact' regime. Watch for a break of the 100-pip range to trigger a spec long unwind.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Technical Correction + Retail Extreme
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.3899 stalls its weekly upside run (+0.84%) as oil prices retreat (WTI $92.3, -3.91%). A massive retail divergence is active with 84% of retail traders short, fading the institutional USD bid. While the +150bp carry advantage (Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25%) and positive CAD CESI (+4) support the pair, technicals indicate a correction toward support as the upside momentum exhausts. Implication: retail capitulation risk remains high if 1.3950 is tested, but near-term mean reversion is favored.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Volatility Premium + Real Rate Headwinds
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1624 maintains an elevated +3.3% Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.1% vs HV30 3.8%), signaling institutional demand for downside protection despite a quiet 1D change (+0.19%). The real rate differential of 149bp (USD +1.23% vs EUR -0.26%) continues to favor the USD structurally. With COT LF net short -26,311 and a mild -0.32 25d RR put skew, the path of least resistance remains lower. Watch the ECB meeting on 11 Jun for any shift in the accommodative real rate stance.
